aaData.rawDescription:
Open doors, gates, locks, and electric strikes. Remote control and closure status monitoring.
Remote access control for doors and gates. Checking lock status.
Control of doors and gates via smartphone or computer from anywhere in the world
Real-time status monitoring of doors, gates, or locks
Smartphone notifications (e.g. when the door is opened)
Schedule with astronomical clock (e.g. control at sunrise or sunset)
Ability to control other BleBox controllers (e.g. lighting, doorbell)
Safe operating voltage: 12–24V AC/DC
Volt-free relay (5–30V DC / 5–230V AC) ideal for electric strikes, locks, and bolts
Two additional control inputs 12–24V AC/DC (e.g. door release button, doorbell)
Optional control via direct or local connection
Additionally:
Ability to share the controller with other people via the free BleBox cloud
Option to create a wireless, powered WiFi button
Option to create a wireless, battery-powered uWiFi button
Ability to pair rechargeable and battery-powered uWiFi wireless remotes
Voice control and integrations:
Control of doors and wickets
The “BleBox doorBox” WiFi controller,
thanks to its volt-free relay (5–30V DC / 5–230V AC),
allows you to open and close doors, wickets, and similar installations
equipped with an electric strike, magnetic lock, electric lock, or bolt – from anywhere in the world.
The free wBox app for smartphones and computers
enables the creation of groups and scenes, allowing simultaneous
control of multiple BleBox devices.
Directly, locally, or remotely
“BleBox doorBox” offers three modes of control:
Direct
– via the WiFi network broadcast by the controller itself
Local
– through the home WiFi network, without Internet access
Remote
– from anywhere in the world, thanks to the free BleBox cloud (servers located in the European Union, USA, and Canada)
Control is performed using a smartphone or computer.
Thanks to the encrypted WiFi network that the controller can broadcast
while simultaneously being connected to the home WiFi,
you are guaranteed operation even in the event of a router, WiFi access point, or Internet failure.
Enjoy smart home solutions stress-free — you can open the door even if you forget your keys or if your network equipment or Internet connection goes down.
Control opening status
The “BleBox doorBox” WiFi controller allows you to connect a sensor that enables real-time monitoring in the wBox app to check whether a door or gate is open or closed.
The controller input supports connection of:
A volt-free “NO” type reed switch
An inductive sensor of type “NO” / “NPN”
This lets you check the open/closed status at any time, no matter where you are.
You no longer have to worry whether the door or gate was left open.
Phone notifications
The
“BleBox doorBox”
door controller can
send notifications
directly to your phone.
Example notifications:
when a door or gate is opened
when a door or gate is closed
when the door release button is used
when the doorbell button is pressed
when any signal is detected on the additional input
Notifications can include your own personalized message!*
*Feature coming soon via a software update.
Scheduling and astronomical clock
Scheduling with a built-in astronomical clock
enables automatic control of the device
at a specific time or based on sunrise and sunset,
taking your geographic location into account. The operation time can be precisely adjusted to your individual needs.
This allows for automatic opening and closing of doors or gates at specific times:
For an electric lock operating in step-by-step mode, the controller sends an impulse to open or close at the selected time or time of day.
For a continuously operated electric strike, the controller can open and maintain the open state, then close it at the chosen time or time of day.*
*The “open and hold” function will be available soon via a software update. Currently, only pulse control is supported.
Safe voltage!
The WiFi controller “BleBox doorBox” is powered by a
safe voltage of 12–24V AC/DC,

Voice control and integrations:
Control of doors and wickets
The “BleBox doorBox PRO” WiFi controller,
thanks to its volt-free relay (5–30V DC / 5–230V AC),
allows you to open and close doors, wickets
, and similar installations equipped with an electric strike, magnetic lock, electric lock, or bolt —
from anywhere in the world.
The free wBox app for smartphones and computers
enables the creation of groups and scenes, allowing simultaneous
control of multiple BleBox devices.
Directly, locally, or remotely
“BleBox doorBox PRO” offers three modes of control:
Direct
– via the WiFi network broadcast by the controller itself
Local
– through the home WiFi network, without Internet access
Remote
– from anywhere in the world, thanks to the free BleBox cloud
(servers located in the European Union, USA, and Canada)
Control is performed using a smartphone or computer.
Thanks to the encrypted WiFi network that the controller can broadcast
while simultaneously being connected to the home WiFi,
you are guaranteed operation even in the event of a router, WiFi access point, or Internet failure.
Enjoy smart home solutions stress-free — you can open the door even if you forget your keys or if your network equipment or Internet connection goes down.
Control opening status
The “BleBox doorBox PRO” WiFi controller allows you to connect a sensor that enables real-time monitoring in the wBox app to check whether a door or gate is open or closed.
The controller input supports connection of:
A volt-free “NO” type reed switch
An inductive sensor of type “NO” / “NPN”
This lets you check the open/closed status at any time, no matter where you are.
You no longer have to worry whether the door or gate was left open.
Phone notifications
The “BleBox doorBox PRO”
door controller can
send notifications
directly to your phone.
Example notifications:
when a door or gate is opened
when a door or gate is closed
when the door release button is used
when the doorbell button is pressed
when any signal is detected on the additional input
Notifications can include your own personalized message!*
*Feature coming soon via a software update.
Scheduling and astronomical clock
Scheduling with a built-in astronomical clock
enables automatic control of the device
at a specific time or based on sunrise and sunset
, taking your geographic location into account. The operation time can be precisely adjusted to your individual needs.
This allows for automatic opening and closing of doors or gates at specific times:
For an electric lock operating in step-by-step mode, the controller sends an impulse to open or close at the selected time or time of day.
For a continuously operated electric strike, the controller can open and maintain the open state, then close it at the chosen time or time of day.*

Light sensor, illuminance sensor (lux, lx), luxmeter, photometer. Charts, notifications, control actions!
ADDITIONALLY:: Miniature dimensions allowing easy integration of the sensor Simple installation using the included double-sided tape or 2 screws Plug&Play – can be connected to a 5V charger (USB-A connector) Can be connected to a 12V or 24V DC power supply Can be used to create a weather station with other BleBox sensors Voice control and integrations:
WIFI LIGHT SENSOR: “BleBox luxSensor” is a WiFi sensor measuring light intensity in the range of 0 to 50,000 lx (lux) – the so-called luxmeter, photometer, solar sensor, light sensor, brightness sensor, light meter. The sensor communicates via a local WiFi network (it can work without Internet access) or directly (creating its own WiFi network). Thanks to the free BleBox cloud and the wBox application (available for smartphones and computers), remote communication from anywhere in the world is also possible.
DETAILED DATA!: The “BleBox luxSensor” offers a wide measurement range from 0 to 50,000 lx (lux) and a high resolution of 0.1 lx (lux) in the range of 0–10 lx (lux), which allows for precise determination of the brightness level. It can be used indoors to monitor whether the brightness of the lighting is appropriate for the activities performed or outdoors to assess atmospheric conditions and the current time of day. For example: – night, – twilight, – total cloudiness, – cloudy, – clear sky, – cloudless sky.
LIGHTING CONTROL: The “BleBox luxSensor” can be connected to other “BleBox” controllers to: automatically control lighting on/off (e.g. “switchBox” , “switchBox DIN” , “switchBox DC” ) automatically control the brightness of dimmable lighting (e.g. “dimmerBox” , “dacBoxD DC” , “ wLightBox” , “wLightBoxS” ) Examples of automation scenarios: Automatic control of lighting brightness in home or service premises (office, bar, restaurant, living room). Automatic control of lighting of house or greenhouse plants, taking into account current weather conditions. Automatic control of outdoor lighting, which, unlike an astronomical clock, takes into account current weather conditions affecting brightness. The device can wirelessly send up to 30 control commands to other BleBox devices via a local WiFi network (regardless of the Internet connection).
SHADE CONTROL: Connect the “BleBox luxSensor” to the “shutterBox” , “shutterBox DIN” lub “shutterBoxDC” , controller to automatically regulate the level of shading by adjusting the percentage of opening of blinds, roller blinds or awnings. For example: Automatic control of blind slats to limit the amount of light in the room (e.g. when watching TV or working on a computer). Automatic control of external blinds to limit heating of rooms from the sun. Automatic control of pergola slats, awnings or reflectors to disperse sharp sunlight. The device can wirelessly send up to 30 control commands to other BleBox devices via a local WiFi network (regardless of the Internet connection).
CHARTS AND HISTORICAL DATA: In the wBox application, you can view historical measurement values ​​on an interactive graph. The sensor provides two values ​​in the selected time window: average illuminance value, maximum illuminance value. This data helps determine whether the sky was cloudless or clear during a given time window. Historical data also helps track and determine how much light reached crops or photovoltaic panels. For advanced users, there is an option to export data to files in “.csv” or “.xlsx” (Excel) formats, which allows for further processing or archiving of information.
NOTIFICATIONS, WARNINGS: The “BleBox luxSensor” sunlight sensor can send push notifications to your smartphone. The notification can be sent as a warning after exceeding a certain brightness threshold or as a cyclical weather message. The content of the notification is fully configurable!
WATERPROOF AND FUNCTIONAL HOUSING: The housing of the “BleBox luxSensor” brightness

The proxiSwitch controller is equipped with a capacitive sensor
and algorithms thanks to which

Invisible button
Thanks to the high sensitivity
of the proxiSwitch controller,
you can hide it under a kitchen counter, on the side of a wardrobe, in a wall or even in the middle of another object!
Only you know where it is hidden
and where you need to touch to control!
Automatic calibration
Thanks to special algorithms, the controller adjusts the sensitivity itself, depending on the environment in which you use it.
The maximum thickness through which the controller will recognize the touch or closeness of the hand is about 3 cm (for chipboard, furniture board, solid wood). Furniture with a “honeycomb cross section” (air spaces) should be avoided.
4 operating modes!
The proxiSwitch controller can operate in 4 modes:
bistable:
switches the state after each close-up / touch of the hand
bistable with memory:
switches the state after each approach / touch, additionally remembers its state after a power failure
monostable NO:
activates the output when it detects a hand approach or touch. The maximum pulse time is approximately 5 seconds
monostable NC:
turns off the output when it detects a hand approach or touch. The maximum pulse time is approximately 5 seconds
Compatibility with WiFi drivers
The proxiSwitch controller can also be used as a touch button for other BleBox controllers.
Connect the proxiSwitch output to the button input of DC controllers (12-24V) to control them by touching or bringing your hand closer
